mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equities by lowering discount rates and refinancing stress, so S&P 500 forward 20-day returns turn positive when the policy rate is in a clear downtrend.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not positive in a majority of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the sign of the return response is opposite in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent baseline signals weakening household income and demand, which compresses earnings expectations and pushes the S&P 500 lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional average by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized acceleration in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broad demand expansion, supporting revenue growth and margin resilience, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in PAYEMS and RSAFS fail to outperform single-variable conditions, or if the interaction term is not positive in a rolling 5-year test with p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rising INDPRO without a matching rise in CPIAUCSL indicates improving real activity with contained inflation pressure, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising INDPRO and flat-to-falling CPIAUCSL do not show positive excess 20-day returns relative to other periods, or if the joint effect changes sign across three consecutive rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is accelerating while FEDFUNDS remains low, real policy rates are negative and inflation expectations rise faster than the policy response, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/low-rate triggered periods do not deliver negative 20-day returns on average, or if SPX rises in more than half of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST marks tightening housing demand and weaker cyclical spending, which tends to precede negative S&P 500 performance over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% from its level 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns are not negative on average after the trigger, or if the trigger does not precede underperformance relative to non-trigger months in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE signals labor market deterioration that reduces future earnings expectations and should lower the S&P 500 over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-UNRATE signal fails to outperform either series alone, or if triggered negative-labor episodes do not show below-baseline 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising GDPC1 and RSAFS together indicate durable real demand normalization, which should produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if recoveries with rising GDPC1 and RSAFS do not produce positive 60-day returns versus baseline, or if the interaction effect is not stable across rolling 4-quarter windows.
